headline: Article 49A's expulsion carve-out, blocked from court by the Societies Act
context: Article 49A(2)(c) of the Federal Constitution exempts expulsion from triggering loss of an MP's seat. Section 18C of the Societies Act 1966 bars courts from hearing party-membership disputes. The two produce a constitutional protection courts cannot practically interpret.

cards.big: A Labuan MP's appeal over the cessation of his Bersatu membership was dismissed at the Court of Appeal on 29 April 2026.
cards.sub: Article 49A(2)(c) of the Federal Constitution exempts expulsion from triggering loss of MP status. After 3 years 7 months, no court has interpreted the carve-out on its merits.

cards.big: Article 49A inserted by Constitution (Amendment) (No. 3) Act 2022 (Act A1663), in force 5 October 2022.
cards.sub: The text prevents a party from causing an MP's seat to be vacated merely by expelling that MP.

cards.big: Zuraida Kamaruddin's case (CoA December 2024) was decided on Contracts Act s.75 proportionality, not Article 49A.
cards.sub: The court held the RM10 million bond disproportionate and reduced compensation to RM100,000. The breach was upheld. PKR's Federal Court appeal hears 16 July 2026.

cards.big: Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi (May 2026) used Article 51 to vacate Pandan and Setiawangsa before leaving PKR.
cards.sub: Resign seat first, leave party second. The Election Commission treated the vacancies as Article 51 resignations and stated no by-elections under Article 54(1)'s two-year window.

cards.big: Article 49A(2)(c) names a constitutional protection. Section 18C of the Societies Act tells the courts not to reach it. Two laws, one shielded carve-out.
Like a constitutional door locked from the other side by a different law.
cards.sub: Article 49A(2)(c) is the protection. Section 18C of the Societies Act 1966 keeps courts from disputes that could interpret it.

cards.big: Article 49A(2)(c) is a constitutional protection Section 18C makes unreachable. Until the two laws align or a case finds a procedural path, the carve-out's scope stays speculative.
